description_long: This shrub consists of several tall, slender green stalks with elongated, blade-like leaves radiating from the base. Prominently, it features two to three upright brown cylindrical flower spikes, characteristic of cattail plants (Typha species). The leaves are narrow, flexible, and arching, with a smooth matte surface and a vibrant green color. The brown flower spikes are dense and velvety, contrasting with the green foliage. The base of the shrub is dense with overlapping leaves, while the upper portion is more open, showcasing the vertical spikes. This plant is commonly found in wetland or park environments, often near water features.
description: A cluster of tall, green, blade-like leaves with upright brown cylindrical flower spikes, resembling a cattail plant. The shrub has a dense leafy base and prominent vertical spikes.
description_view_0: Two brown flower spikes and several arching green leaves are visible, with the foliage appearing dense at the base.
description_view_1: Three brown flower spikes are visible, with the tallest spike at the center and leaves splaying outward.
description_view_2: Two main brown flower spikes are seen, with the leaves appearing more upright and less spread out.
description_view_3: The tallest brown flower spike is most prominent, with the leaves appearing more vertical and less dense at the base.
